name: Lattice ECP5 45K-CABGA381
description: Lattice ECP5 (LFE5UM-45F) in CABGA-381 package with dual SERDES channels for PCIe Gen1 x1. Mid-range FPGA optimized for low cost, small form factor, and low power consumption.

fabric.notes: ECP5 PFU blocks contain 4 slices, each slice has a LUT4 and a FF. LUT4 count and FF count are from the ECP5 Family Data Sheet Table 1.1.

bram.notes: sysMEM embedded block RAM. Each DP16KD block provides 16 Kbits of data storage plus 2 Kbits of parity (18 Kbits total). Supports single-port, dual-port, and pseudo-dual-port modes.

dsp.notes: sysDSP slices. Each slice contains two 18x18 multipliers. DDR mode doubles effective throughput. 56 slices yield 112 MULT18X18D primitives. Max DSP frequency from ECP5 Family Data Sheet Section 2.9.

clocking.notes: 4 sysCLOCK PLLs with programmable frequency synthesis. Max VCO frequency 800 MHz.

io.notes: 245 user I/Os for LFE5UM-45F in BG381 package. From ECP5 Family Data Sheet Table 1.3.

serdes.notes: 2 SERDES channels at up to 3.2 Gbps each (ECP5, non-5G). ECP5-5G variants support up to 5 Gbps. From ECP5 Product Brief and SerDes/PCS User Guide FPGA-TN-02206.

pcie.notes: PCIe Gen1 x1 via embedded SERDES. Theoretical BW = 2.5 GT/s × (8/10) = 2.0 Gbps = 250 MB/s. Practical BW ~80% due to TLP overhead, MPS/MRRS settings, and protocol framing. DMA latency measured from Lattice PCI Express Scatter-Gather DMA demo (UG106). Max payload and read request sizes from PCIe IP Core User Guide.

power.notes: Power estimates from Lattice Power Consumption TN-02210. Static power is quiescent at 25°C. Dynamic power is for a moderately utilized design (~50% toggle rate). Actual power varies with design complexity, clock frequency, and I/O configuration.

synthesis.notes: Open-source synthesis via Yosys + nextpnr-ecp5 (Project Trellis). Typical Fmax of 100-150 MHz for moderately complex designs. Resource type names match nextpnr utilization report keys.
